reviews.review_description: The top review I saw pinned called this ‘game’ - “exceptional work” and I 100% agree. This is not a ‘game’ per se but an audio-visual experience.  It’s like putting on the dark side of the moon and the wizard of oz - except it syncs much better and, in many many many ways, is much more impressive.  If you are looking for an experience, this game does not dissappoint. I don’t play it often, but I come back to it once every couple of months and my mind is blown every time.

Why was Visionarium 1 removed?
If I want to download it, do I have to get it only from Steam?
Is there any other way to install it directly on a standalone headset?

developer_response.body:
Hi, Visionarium 1 was never removed, there simply is no version ever developed of the visionarium 1 version you see on steam to run only on the quest.. Versions on steam require a PC.
This version is the only version that runs on quest stand alone devices. I hope this clears things up.

reviews.review_description: I was fascinated by this project. Thank you to the author for this exceptional work. I really enjoy it.

I absolutely love this app. When I first started watching it, the art was not what I was familiar with, and it is not as intricate as some other others, but the more I have watched it the more I enjoy it. The eastern art and 360° experience Are outstanding. I enjoy the music that’s included, but I also enjoy listening to my own music while standing and surfing through the scenes. If you get the app, use it for a while and give it a try. As I said, at first, I was not highly impressed but the more I have used it the more I like it ￼. It is a somewhat long episode so I frequently will watch parts of it, Stop and then restart and return to previous spot when watching again.

You should see this when it’s run on Quest games optimiser on maximum settings for every option available… mind blowing !!!! Highly recommend, it is definitely 10/10 for the experience, price, shock factor for friends and overall enjoyment, well done 👏
This was a really cool experience and the duration was way longer than I thought it would be. Every time I thought it was over, it kept going. Very highly recommend.

reviews.review_title: Still Playing With It, But I Like It
reviews.review_description: It started working after I uninstalled and reinstalled it on my Meta Quest 3S. I still need to play around with free-flying mode, which I think will be even more fun. I like the alien ship segment and the reverse birth segment the most. I am curious to see how this will influence my dreams.

developer_response.body:
Good to hear that it works now! Thanks for updating the review. Enjoy the experience ;)
(btw freefly mode is most fun when you turn on microphone input and have your own music playing from your computer or speakers. The microphone will pick up the sound)

reviews.review_title: like a travel in your mind
reviews.review_description: absolutely relaxing, best to view while laying on a couch / ground with in ears. great job!

You guys did a great job with this version I loved it!!!...I also have your 3 games on  Steam for my Vive....I wish I could combine your stuff with TRIPP. That would be absolute heaven.....keep up the great work....your style is spot on in my world. :)

I recieved this product for free in a giveaway, and it's very cool. I'd definetly reccommend playing this while laying on a couch or bed as it feels way more immersive. It's super cool to float through these magical and trippy environments and just take it all in. I really enjoyed playing it while listening to audiobooks and podcasts. It's worth buying if it's what you're looking for, but don't expect some crazy gameplay.

I’m into VR for the visual and audio experiences and I’m not much of a gamer so this type of app appeals to me. I’m pretty sensitive to motion sickness and I was able to get through the whole journey with no problem at all. I enjoyed the flow and transitions throughout and the music was very pleasant. I found it to be exhilarating and awe inspiring and the interactive part was pretty cool. this is another VR experience that will go into my library and I look forward to exploring it many times over. I think it’s well worth the small investment.

This game is best described for myself as a cross between a Star Trek: TMP (the first movie) V'GER movie experience in VR, and a steroid-based representation of an old Steve Ditko drawn Dr. Strange comic book, with a touch of 2001: A Space Odyssey.

The default game mode takes you on an 'on-rails' experience to where you basically sit/stand and look around at the trippy visuals with soothing background music. Turning in this game is limited to snap turning only, which is disappointing (although physical turning can compensate for it).

There is an alternate mode (FreeFly) to where you get off the rails, and can move around at your own pace and any available vantage point, but with 2 disappointing side effects: (i) All music stops (which is a huge immersion breaker) and (ii) any directional control is limited to where you are looking (I would have preferred joystick control for up/down/forward/back for a greater sense of free flight).
I did have one occurrence to where choosing FreeFly resulted in my completely losing all camera control, and put into an almost virtual tailspin (which was slightly nauseating, but pretty cool in its own way).  Turning FreeFly on and off again corrected this issue.

The visuals, in my opinion, don't match the Steam VR version (on which this game is based, and which I have played in the past) in terms of sharpness specifically, but over time I adjusted to that difference, and found the graphics enjoyable, but don't really know how they compare based on other Quest offerings.

Under Options, you have a choice of Visual Display with options of Linear, Decibel and SQRT (square root).  I wish I knew what those choices meant, and how they impact the experience.  You also have a microphone option which apparently allows you to hear your voice in the game; I guess that's a way to pipe in other music during FreeFly, although I would have much preferred an option to include my own music.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the elaborate feedback. Because of the limitation in performance power of the quest, I had to make some sacrifices, which is why the steam version is more detailed. I also didn't manage to find a way of implementing custom music tracks, it would take a different approach than the PC version. But it is definitely something I want to look at in a next update. 
For now, you could try activating the mic. Then play some music from your speakers and set the audio sensitivity to very high and see the particle streams react to it.
The different buttons (linear, decibel and SQRT) are different modes in which the visuals react to the soundtrack or your microphone. Think about how the sound is translated. You will find that some settings will result in more choppy effects while others are more fluid. 
I have put the controls in the free fly mode on my list for future improvement, thanks for pointing that out.

reviews.review_description: The experience of this app is amazing. It's so detailed and well made. It's like you're in your own world and it's nice that you can sit down and enjoy the experience. There are a lot of colours and the music is well chosen.

Great visuals, I really enjoyed the PCVR version.  It doesn't look quite as good, but worth the trade-off for Quest.  If you're at all interested in anything psychedelic, buy it now.

25% off links for other games: https://vr.miketunes.com/discount
